title: Cozy Dense Vietnamese Oat Stir-fry
description: A cozy and dense vegan dish featuring roasted oats with umami flavors, perfect for a satisfying breakfast or light lunch.

ingredients.name: 1 cup quick-cooking oat groats
ingredients.notes: Soaked in hot water for at least 30 minutes

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon fish sauce (optional, use vegan alternative if needed)

instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Rinse oat groats and soak them in hot water for at least 30 minutes.
Drain and pat dry. In a bowl, mix oat groats with soy sauce and fish sauce (if using), then spread evenly on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until golden brown and crispy. Remove from oven and let cool slightly.
In a large skillet, heat some vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add roasted oat groats to the pan, tossing gently with tongs to coat evenly.
Add minced garlic and black pepper to the skillet. Stir-fry for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Gently fold in julienned carrot and sliced green onion into the oat mixture. Cook for an additional 2 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Remove from heat, stir in chopped cilantro leaves if using. Serve warm as a breakfast dish with scrambled tofu, poached eggs, or any other complementary ingredients.
For vegan cheese or sour cream topping, blend cashew halves with lemon juice and nutritional yeast to achieve desired consistency.
